description_long: This object is a double-barreled shotgun with a classic design, featuring two parallel barrels of equal length finished in a dark, matte metal. The receiver and grip are constructed from a wood-textured material, with the grip shaped ergonomically for handheld use and a visible hammer mechanism at the rear. The shotgun has two distinct triggers, each protected by a rounded, elongated trigger guard that accommodates two fingers. The barrels are joined closely together, with no visible ribbing or venting between them, and the overall build is compact, lacking a full shoulder stock. The finish of the wood is smooth with a slight sheen, while the metal barrels appear matte and robust. This type of shotgun is typically used for close-range applications and is often seen in urban or civilian settings.
description: A compact, double-barreled shotgun with a wooden grip and dark metal barrels, featuring dual triggers and a rounded trigger guard. The design is robust and ergonomic, suitable for handheld use.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the double barrels, wooden grip, dual triggers, and hammer mechanism.
description_view_1: Top-down view highlighting the parallel alignment of the barrels and the smooth, contoured wooden grip.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the same double-barrel and grip features, with clear visibility of the trigger guard and hammer.
description_view_3: Barrel-end view showing the twin barrel openings and the compact width of the shotgun.
attribute_barrel_configuration: side-by-side double-barrel, no visible ribbing or venting, barrels of equal length
attribute_trigger_guard_design: elongated, rounded trigger guard sized for two fingers, with smooth contours and no visible reinforcement
